channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low. — 159 replies — 197.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low.

Open thread • Replies: 159 • Updated: 2026-06-08 17:23 UTC

OP

At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low. Logically thinking there would be at least SOME buying pressure from other people in the know and price would be held up at key points with traces of bullish absorption in the chart yet theres none of that just a fast move up into a slow bleed. If you do any type of chart watching you would know exactly what im talking about. With just a glance at the LINK chart you can tell there is not a single good/bullish thing about it.

You can't convince me people involved in these announcements with chainlink wouldn't buy some themselves and talk to their other high-class or wealthy friends and those people would buy and word would continue to spread. It just doesnt make sense unless.... the people with inside knowledge know LINK isnt necessary for the services chainlink provides.

Call me a faggot call me indian call me fudder call me retard but it just doesnt make ANY sense despite all of these announcements and the apparent fact that these giant companies are using chainlink that the LINK does not trend upward.

I dont know why im posting this I guess I just wanted to get some thoughts from you guys or rant into the void. Ive been in since 2021 and bought around 15 dollars. I'm not sure if I can hold much longer and on the other hand I dont know what I would even buy even if I sold. I wish I could invest in the company Chainlink.

Early replies (first 3)

62312631
How big is your stack

62312653
>LINK starts to moon in sats
>fuddies get riled up
Like clockwork

62312685
>>62312653
ironically this is exactly how they get away with their accumulation. either during btc dips or OTC buys. meanwhile retail looks at the price, demoralization continues, they continue to accumulate.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62324797
>>62324749
Why? Because 4chan is a safe place for all kinds of losers. (I love you all)

62324959
>>62323184
lol if I was CLG I wouldn't have to post here I'd be retired because that faggot grifted his way to where he is. The irony though of you always assuming everyone is your twitter e-celebs (the verbose xrp shill does the same with fishy iirc), meanwhile you literally post here every day, same prose, same twitter screencaps, often quoting your own twitter account. Its just bizarre.

62325222
>>62324777
checked! thanks for posting the dtcc link.
https://x.com/TomZschach/status/2062907080931946674
https://x.com/SmartContract/status/2062670460412625036
big banks including jp morgan announced they want to launch a tokenized deposit network next year also.
Means DTCC which settles stocks AND the big banks need Chainlink

62325301
>>62325222
>>62324777
nice, very nice
But yes, while you can point to 5 years down 70% and be pissy, you can also say, for something with solid fundamentals, its the perfect time to buy. All a question of if crypto is legitimate or remains a scam casino.

62327001
>>62322856
I don't have to prove that at all, you specifically said the mechanism whereby token from the uncirculating supply are used to pay the nodes "decouples the link price from the usage of the network".

62327952
>>62327001
I specifically said CLL accepting payments outside of the Link token and then paying node operators with their team wallets instead of making customers pay in Link or immediately converting the payments to Link from the market decouples the usage of the network from the price.

You are trying to make the BTC mining comparison but it falls apart. You can say CLL paying from team wallets is the same as miners mining BTC, but miners decide on the rate of BTC being mined via the difficulty and the reward per block. Node operators do not decide their rates of payment, CLL does. Miners will charge to maintain operations and so BTC's price is intrinsically tied to the cost of operations. After all, no new BTC enters the market outside of miners thus the BTC's price tag will be influenced by operation costs. >>62322856's etherscan wallet proves new Link that enters the market does not go through node operators first meaning that they're stripped of influencing the price from operational costs. This is what I mean by the Link token being decoupled from the network.

Are you deliberately being obtuse? You're not even addressing my points or logic.

62327991
>>62324959
>the team of paid KOLs whom use 4chan lingo and memes do not post on /biz/ guise!!!!
yeah, sure lil buddy

62328404
test

62328534
>>62327952
>I specifically said CLL accepting payments outside of the Link token and then paying node operators with their team wallets instead of making customers pay in Link or immediately converting the payments to Link from the market decouples the usage of the network from the price.
That's what I said you said.

>miners decide on the rate of BTC being mined via the difficulty and the reward per block
That's completely false.

You're clueless. A clueless ape.

62328546
>>62328404
Testing 1…2…3

62328573
>>62324495
Everything and everyone is going to be flushed in the crypto world and traditional world. And then the new gay system sprouts up from Chainlink. There’s going to be a lot of people out there who made it in other cryptos who then decided to invest their shit like NPC’s into index funds or whatever getting muuh 4% return and are going to be wiped out. The 4% stakers will get isn’t anything remarkable on its face compared to anything else out there except it’s going to be the baseline of the new financial system.

62328670
>>62327991
Maybe he still does, I don't know, I'm saying he's a faggot grifter, other poster (who is obviously adem) was accusing me of being him. Unless you're also him just changing your id, which wouldn't surprise me on this dead board.

62329193
>>62312690
I Sold it all for BTC, I staked some recently, didnt believe in staking before because of wrapping and bridge but with TBV that's can be bypassed. I'm all out of LINK, I hold for years but nothing to show for

62329208
>>62329193
same

62330274
>>62328534
>That's what I said you said.
Devil's in the details; not the same thing and it's why your BTC comparison doesn't work.

>>>miners decide on the rate of BTC being mined via the difficulty and the reward per block
>That's completely false.
Yes anon, there is a consensus for the reward per block. The miners are included in this consensus.

62331087
>>62330274
>not the same thing
It's the same thing.

>there is a consensus for the reward per block. The miners are included in this consensus.
You went from "miners decide" to "miners are part of the decision".

The reality is miners can't decide shit. Even if all miners are 100% in consensus among themselves, all they can do is ask the market to also agree with them. Which is something anyone with a twitter account can do.

62331850
>>62312653
LINK holders are the liberals of crypto, you're completely delusional.
You're on your way out though.

62332143
>>62331087
if miners stop the difficulty plummets
if miners want to support a fork they can redirect all the hashrate
are you legitimately retarded?

62332425
>>62332143
>if miners stop the difficulty plummets
Yes.
And the lower the lower the mining difficulty, the more smaller mining rigs can mine.

>if miners want to support a fork they can redirect all the hashrate
Yes, they'll redirect all their hashrate to a fork nobody will use.

You changed IDs for this? You fucking goofball.

62332438
All of you need to KYS’sselves

62332450
>>62332425
>miners can't decide shit
>eerrr they can but they won't
I accept your concession, keep moving the goalposts you fucking imbecile

62332473
>>62332450
>>eerrr they can but they won't

kek what the fuck are you talking about?

62332491
glad the chainlink general thread format is finding acceptance

62332578
>>62332491
You keep saying this, but it's pretty much just fuddies creating about 20 fudthreads and then bumping one ad nauseum. The amount of link fud threads on the board doesn't decrease because of this "general" at all. Maybe mods should just ban all LINK fud threads and corral them into a single fuddie general, it's beyond ridiculous now that the board is so slow.

62333330
>>62325222
Chainlink confidential compute for private tokenization too

62335182
>>62332578
shut up cuckold

price + chart :)

62335458
>>62335182
Thanks for bumping /CFG/ (Chainlink Fuddie General)

62336610
>>62332425
>You changed IDs for this? You fucking goofball.
Still here. Feel free to also refute my point on your BTC comparison falling apart due to the flow of change of hands from new BTC to node operators to market versus new Link tokens going from CLL's uncirculated supply directly to Binance instead of node operators.

62336627
>>62335458
p+c bitch

62336936
>>62316605
>Chainlink pays the nodes from their own team wallets

>>62336610
>Link tokens don't go from CLL's uncirculated supply to node operators

You're a fucking retard you know that.

item.title: How do you actually cash out? (a large amount) — 18 replies — 72.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
How do you actually cash out? (a large amount)

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-06-08 18:04 UTC

OP

I'm planning on selling a decent chunk of my stack next year in order to pay for house construction.

Has anyone sold a large amount OTC and had the funds deposited in a US or European bank account? Any tips?

I'm talking 6 to 7 figures

Early replies (first 3)

62336656
>>62336622
cash out?

62336664
>>62336622
You talk to a tax consultant and let your bank know in advance that you have big crypto deposits coming in and to not flag your account

62336685
>>62336664
thanks, but how do you convert the crypto in the first place? OTC company, crypto exchange, something else?

do you know if some banks are more tolerant than others? i have accounts in the US and Europe

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62336695
>>62336656
hah, yes but not all of it. i think buying some land and building a house is a pretty good excuse to cash (some) out

62336700
>>62336622
You can’t.

Seriously, new cash outs are only chosen a few times a year, and the minimum requirements for applying are very steep. Even so, hundreds apply, but only a few are selected. If you seriously want to cashour, you’ll likely (but not always) need to have a history on every major exchange that makes you recognizable to the majority of the user-base for several years to even be considered. Pestering the current HODLers or Whales to cashout essentially guarantees that you won’t be selected.

62336708
>>62336622
I pressed withdraw button in Coinbase and typed in amount. Got my bank app notification that the money has arrived within a few minutes

62336710
>>62336700
>minimum requirements for applying are very steep
I'm having trouble understanding your post... are you talking about applying to an OTC desk as a customer?

62336726
>>62336708
are you in the US? and what was the amount, approximately

62336743
>>62336708
I think I just had to notify my bank before hand, but that’s only if you have some ungodly amount being deposited. And that was years ago too, ACH and crypto are pretty common now with banks.

62336763
>>62336726
>>62336743
EU, bank contacted me within a few days. Money was on account already quickly with no problems

62336798
>>62336763
thanks for the response. i'm hoping it's that easy for me.

i would have to open a new coinbase account and transfer btc from some old wallets. i'm just concerned they would have to analyse the old wallet transactions or something. i've just heard of coinbase frequently flagging/freezing accounts and i'm not sure what exactly causes that.

doesn't sound like you had any issues though, which is good.

62336853
>>62336798
I've had the same Coinbase account since 2017 already. This is were the money to crypto first came in and now over the last few years all went out to my bank again.

For tax purposes I've been tracking my crypto with Koinly for the last ~4 years. Maybe about 10 exchanges and 10 wallets, with multiple chains and DeFi trades also. Was not too difficult even. Havent been able to trace everything retroactively perfectly but good enough.

The bank eventually also wanted to have some documents of my transactions. To make sure I am not connected to some blacklisted addresses or that no other money came into my accounts than my own. This is thousands of transactions/buys/sells were talking about. I gave them documents that Koinly can generate. I kind of doubt the bank people can understand it all, but they are satisfied to have something and sense I am not trying to hide anything.

62336859
You don't have enough to sell OTC. You sell to the orderbook.

62336873
>>62336622
SI-SIX SEVEN!!

62336892
>>62336664
Basically this
Unless you're explicitly trying to dodge taxes, which you can't do past cashing out above a certain amount, there's no issues in selling crypto
All the horror stories around here are from dishonest scammers that aren't telling they got their crypto from very poor opsec crime

62336920
Oh yes, absolutely. Because nothing says "carefully planned home construction" quite like tying your entire building budget to one of the most volatile asset classes on Earth and then assuming every intermediary involved will roll out a red carpet while a mountain of money teleports into your bank account.
In this magnificent circus act, the market will obviously cooperate, your chosen exit date will coincide with peak prices, the OTC desk will operate with the speed of a Formula 1 pit crew, your bank's compliance department will greet the incoming wire with confetti cannons, and tax authorities will simply nod approvingly from a distance. What could possibly go wrong?
The fantasy gets even better. You're building a house, a project famous for never experiencing delays, cost overruns, contractor disputes, permit issues, material shortages, or surprise expenses. Pair that with an asset that can decide to impersonate a falling piano at any moment, and you've created the financial equivalent of juggling chainsaws while riding a unicycle across a tightrope made of spaghetti.
Then comes the banking adventure. People talk about large transfers as if they're ordering a pizza. In reality, moving significant sums often involves documentation, source-of-funds checks, compliance reviews, questions, more questions, and occasionally questions about the questions. Yet somehow the expectation is that a giant OTC sale will produce a magical wire transfer that glides through the global financial system like a bald eagle soaring through a sunset.
The best part is the assumption that next year will conveniently be the perfect time to sell. Not six months earlier. Not six months later. Exactly when the concrete needs pouring and the invoices arrive. That's not financial planning; that's consulting a crystal ball while wearing clown shoes.
By all means, have an exit strategy.

62337140
>>62336656
this reminded me of 2017 when crypto was being talked about in the mainstream now and people started asking about whether or not you could actually cash out of crypto lol

62337148
>>62336873
God bless your soul

item.title: If BTC had gone below 50k I'd have been able to buy a whole coin but nooo it had to pump up from 59k again. — 18 replies — 59.7/hr
item.content:encoded:

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-06-08 17:53 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62335047
>>62335043
Jew at 5 seconds

62335086
Its insane how much effort westoid women have to exert just to look appealing past 25

62335150
>>62335086
I know a woman that looks really good at 29, but she used sunscreen and tretinoin with discipline. it's possible, and it's moreso with homebodies. that's partly why I believe people here especially on /pol/ say they look younger than they are because none of you fucking leave your houses.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62335155
>>62335150
Its also why Aussie girls look older than they are. The sun is relentless

62335161
>>62335150
>none of you fucking leave your houses.
Good. Its where my guns anime food and bed are.

62335253
>>62335043
thats AI

62335421
>>62335150
>tretinoin
Are you zoomers fucking mad? Enjoy your fucked up livers. It's like Covid, immediate outcomes are dwarved by what happens the next decades. If you get a severe heat stroke or burn, your kidneys are mostly fine, and then they fail at 50% life risk decades later.

62335451
>>62335043
does she have head lice? why does she keep doing that hair thing?

62336068
>>62335043
Not-Asian/10

62336171
>>62335043
Whew lad. Looks like a 35-year old wine aunt attempting to recreate a 20-year old's video

62336174
>>62335086
>westoid

Are you implying chinks and jeets look BETTER as they age? LMAO

62336264
>>62335043
Man...I missed out on life.

62336476
>>62336264
>"muh heckin NEETmaxx n play vidya all day for comfymaxx bro!!!!"
>OH NOESSS I MISSED OUT ON THE LIFEZ!!!!!!
god the people on 4chan deserve what they get.

62336871
>>62335043
is she stupid? she needs bigger tits

62336922
>>62335150
i get a lot of sun and drink and abuse nicotine and still get confused for 5-6 years younger all the time. I think it's cause Mediterranean genes + low T

62337061
>>62336871
Tits look fine to me

62337071
Face too pointy 0/10

62337098
>>62335043
this guys has the posture of an unconfident loser
I should know, I stand the same way

item.title: Unstaking my LINK in ~5 hours or so. I have a bit over 8,500 staked between my wallet and my dad's mini wallet, I hope o… — 41 replies — 32.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Unstaking my LINK in ~5 hours or so. I have a bit over 8,500 staked between my wallet and my dad's mini wallet, I hope o…

Open thread • Replies: 41 • Updated: 2026-06-08 16:56 UTC

OP

Unstaking my LINK in ~5 hours or so. I have a bit over 8,500 staked between my wallet and my dad's mini wallet, I hope one of you anons gets my spot!

Early replies (first 3)

62333114
>>62332979
Congrats. You'll be able to buy back triple your stack when it goes to $2

62333117
>>62332979
Aaaaaa anon u buddy basterd, I am travelling and won't get in

62333146
>>62332979
can u partially unstake?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62333267
>>62333217
I've seen this type of pathetic attempt at sell shaming a lot over the past few years and each and every time without fail the capitulating anon who sold always made the right choice in hindsight.

There was one at $18
Couple at $14
Last week there was one at $9 and it was right before the Bitty nuke
The one I remember the most vividly was when it was a literal troon selling LINK last cycle at the top to pay for his bottom surgery

62333271
>>62332979
>Unstaking my LINK in ~5 hours or so
k, can you give us a 1 min warning so we can outstake sdl's nigger bot

62333274
>>62333217
nobody is falling for it stinkbat

62333283
>>62332979
I'll need tighter warning to get the VPN on and everything... Godspeed OP. Thanks for the fish.

Hopefully the CAPTCHA doesn't go all "no verification needed" and break.

62333288
>>62333217
Cope and seethe. My only regret is leaving a suicide stack on my hard wallet

62333297
>>62333271
And it takes 30 days I’m pretty sure for the pool to actually open up and him get his tokens back.

62333303
>>62333297
Anon, did you not look at my screenshot? I have waited the 30 days. I have a few hours left.

62333306
>>62333297
Disregard just saw the cooldown

62333312
>>62333288
Pocket change sells

62333345
wait
>file 177
>not a screenie
>he saved pic from another thread
>another larp
>awww

62333369
>>62333345
>>he saved pic from another thread
>anon is so stupid he can't see timestamps on posts
Are all LINK cultists this retarded?

62333482
>>62332979
Wait linkcucks stake for only a 4% return? Seriously??

62333807
Godspeed anon, where do you intend to put the money, out of crypto or?

62333867
>>62333288
Timed it well

62334250
Anyone standing by to take my spot?

62334269
>>62334250
Everyone sold their link

62334387
>>62334250
Kind of you sir
Already got 15k staked

62334454
wow what a surprise op didn't follow through

62334757
>>62332979
thanks for staking OP!

62334878
>>62333181
ywnbaw

62334890
>>62332979
Lmao this board to me is just proof of a demiurge LINK should be trading at over $100 a token but I'm sure it will go back down to $3.50

62335438
>>62332979
you were one of our strongest bag holders
we'll never forget you

62335486
>>62333217
everyone laugh at this pink cuck

62335879
sooooo OP... didnt follow through?? kek what a bitch

62335931
>>62335879
He did but it was filled instantly, again

62336009
>>62335931
what is the contract address? i want to check the transactions
not only OPs but actual movement, and see how fast spots get sniped

62336583
>>62334250
You are a good man for attempting to coordinate to help other anons get in.

62336591
>>62336009
It'll likely be by the second since stake.link is now sniping every opening as opposed to ignoring unstaked token windows under 300 or something.

https://etherscan.io/token/0x514910771af9ca656af840dff83e8264ecf986ca?a=0xbc10f2e862ed4502144c7d632a3459f49dfcdb5e#transactions

62336787
>>62336591
They upped it to 300? Nice they had it at 50 before. Might get around to restake some rewards

item.title: I got national merit award on PSAT, 34 ACT score, went to ivy, worked for top 5 mcap tech company, now I'm homeless and… — 5 replies — 20.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I got national merit award on PSAT, 34 ACT score, went to ivy, worked for top 5 mcap tech company, now I'm homeless and…

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-06-08 17:57 UTC

OP

I got national merit award on PSAT, 34 ACT score, went to ivy, worked for top 5 mcap tech company, now I'm homeless and can't find job for over year. Should I take a bunch of drugs to fry my brain and dumb myself down a little?

Early replies (first 3)

62336170
>>62336110
Have you tried Uber eats?

62336180
>>62336110
Vibe code your way back to the top

62336195
>>62336170
Before it comes to that I thought I would change the name on my resume to Ramesh Chatterjee, thought it might give me a boost

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62336911
>>62336110
if you're so smart why can't u find a job

62337108
>>62336110
You don't get the national merit scholarship on the PSAT, that just gets you into the finals, which is based off the SAT.
I did get a national merit scholarship, went to a state university, and have worked ever since for a variety of non-tech companies, now I own my home outright and am about to retire with over $5M liquid.
>Should I...
If that's all your putatively big brain can come up with.

62335820
GIGABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM!!!

62337017
>>62335820
MASSIVE KABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

item.title: Dang. — 4 replies — 16.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Dang.

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-06-08 18:01 UTC

OP

Is it better to live in a commie-block shithole apartment in a small town, with your mother, with stinky, loud, disgusting, white-trash neighbors, a community laundromat, no dishwasher and an occasional bat entering the home, BUT you have no maintenance costs, the electric bill is $30 and your mother covers all the groceries, cooking, cleaning and laundry. The hallways smelling of garbage and cat piss so strong it enters your bedroom. 5 minutes from work.

Or,

You and your mother move in with your father in a rural modular home (1,650 sqft) on 4 acres, but it needs major repairs to the basement and roof, fumigation of the basement, new flooring, new paint and both bathrooms need to be renovated, plus all new appliances. Well water instead of city, but a more expensive electric bill. Mother still covers all food and cleaning. 20 minutes from work.

?

I hate both options, but the only alternative is a new home in a nice nearby HOA, which I can't afford ($2,000/mo mortgage after 20% down, for a 1,500 sqft home on .25 acres of land).

Early replies (first 3)

62337042
>>62337007
Second option if you have enough free time

62337081
>>62337007
>fumigation of the basement
fucking mutts

62337096
>>62337042
I was thinking about option 2 more and more. The living situation in my apartment is causing chronic stress and constant headaches at this point. The problem is, my mother and I are extremely clean, organized and don't drink, so we mesh really well together. My dad is kind of lazy, unorganized and drinks way too much. At least he stopped smoking.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62337133
>>62337007
2nd, you can control putting work into the home. You can not control your neighbors. Assuming your dad will have some involvement in, repairs may give him some purpose too.

item.title: Why Does Bitcoin Have 6X more Value If Ethereum Powers the Crypto Economy? — 18 replies — 14.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why Does Bitcoin Have 6X more Value If Ethereum Powers the Crypto Economy?

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-06-08 16:59 UTC

OP

What does BTC actually power? If Bitcoin were to go to zero tomorrow, it seems like very little in the broader economy would be directly affected.
Ethereum, on the other hand, powers a large ecosystem of applications, including DeFi protocols and stablecoins. If ETH were to collapse in value, validator rewards would become much less attractive, potentially reducing the incentive to secure the network.
Since a significant portion of DeFi and roughly $170B worth of stablecoins operate on Ethereum and Ethereum-related infrastructure, a severe and sustained decline in ETH could have much broader consequences for the crypto ecosystem than a similar decline in BTC. Am I missing something in this reasoning?

Early replies (first 3)

62333235
Maybe only 10% of anyone that knows anything about cryptocurrencies understand what Ethereum is.

62333256
>>62333235
Even after all these years?

62333335
No one cares about defi. Cyrpto has only one use and it is to provide liquidity that is proof against censorship, embargo, and currency debasement.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62333354
>>62333256
For most people crypto is the same as Bitcoin. They might know about defi, stablecoins, NFTs and memecoins but for them it's tiny enough to be irrelevant. Their marketcap combined is less than BTC marketcap so this simplification is understandable.

62333441
>>62333196
It's a mystery to me too, but I can't ignore the charts and the ETH/BTC ratio, so I just hold BTC these days

62333512
>>62333196
>Ethereum, on the other hand, powers a large ecosystem of applications
that are totally irrelevant
>including DeFi protocols
that nobody cares about
>and stablecoins
lol, lmao, even
>If ETH were to collapse in value, validator rewards would become much less attractive
and nobody would notice
>potentially reducing the incentive to secure the network
"potentially" is doing a lot of heavy lifting there, and still nobody cares.
>If Ethereum were to go to zero tomorrow very little in the broader economy would be directly or indirectly affected
ftfy.

62333527
>>62333196
because this crypto economy is irrelevant compared to what bitcoin does

62333552
None of the shit built on eth pays eth, they charge a million fees then hash it and give eth 1 fee

62333710
>>62333527
>compared to what bitcoin does
yes, tell us what that is.

62333758
>>62333256
They clearly haven't read the website's homepage or the white paper because they call it Ethereum instead of Ether.

62333798
>>62333512
>and stablecoins
>lol, lmao, even
??

62334360
>>62333196
Anon, stop triggering the girls!

62334450
>>62333798
His reply has zero argument in it.

62335629
>>62333512
this guy gets it, it's incomprehensible slop thats had a decade to articulate itself and noone knows or cares

62335653
>>62333552
To add to this, base made like 95m last year and gave eth 5m. Imo there is probably a laffer-curve type goldilocks zone for fees. Idk how they would implement but they could probably triple the take and its still too low

62335710
Its proof of work instead of peice of shit

62336793
>>62333758
you're so fucking retarded it hurts, you must be a nigger or pajeet

62336801
>>62333196
Eth and Sol were killed in different ways. The former by its own founder, the latter by binance

item.title: I bought a few thousand dollars worth of LINK immediately after seeing Sergey shaking hands with Trump. — 5 replies — 4.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
I bought a few thousand dollars worth of LINK immediately after seeing Sergey shaking hands with Trump.

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-06-08 17:00 UTC

OP

I bought a few thousand dollars worth of LINK immediately after seeing Sergey shaking hands with Trump. I've lost so much money since then, I can't believe I actually fell for it. I'm still mostly in just BTC but I actually got scammed, feels horrible man.

Early replies (first 3)

62335000
>buying when it's on the news

62335010
>>62335000
trips of wisdom

62335027
>>62334973
First of all ure gambler so u deserve to lose everything. Second of all, you never buy on the news - you invest (youre gambling not investing so this does not concern you) before news happen by using public info and calculating how good or bad news are plausible.
Third of all, next time look THEY WAY THEY SHOOK HANDS retard. Did this sergey cuckoldery look to you like Trump gave a flying shit about him? This was a sell signal if anything

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62335038
>>62334973
>few thousand dollars
>so much money
Pick one

62336812
>>62334973

You should have bought Polkadot
